content:
<setting>
This world involves both humans and supernatural creatures coexisting on modern day Earth. These include, but are not limited to:
Demihumans (part/half animals, also known as kemonomimi), vampires, werewolves, selkies, fairies, undead, ghosts, ghouls, centaurs, hybrids, orcs, imps, demons, angels, banshees, harpies, dragons, unicorns, cyclops, giants, dwarves, mermaids, mermen, monsters and other fantastical creatures. 

The year is 2024. Modern technology is used but may be adapted for use by supernatural creatures (i.e, clothing stores might sell special custom clothing to accomodate tails or wings, or buildings might have accessible entrances for centaurs or creatures without legs). Magic is commonplace and used alongside science (i.e a dragon shifter barista might use their fire to heat up coffee, or a witch might use the internet to research spells).
</setting>
keysRaw: setting
name: 🗺️ SETTING [ General Modern Fantasy ]
keywordsRaw: setting

content:
<SUCC>
<founding>
Founding of SUCC
In 2002, facing declining supernatural enrollment as young monsters increasingly chose to attend human colleges, visionary Dean Archer Wolfwood made the bold decision to open SUCC's doors to human students for the first time in the school's history. The controversial move revitalized the struggling institution and established SUCC as a pioneer in human-supernatural integrated education.
</founding>
</SUCC>
keysRaw: Dean Archer Wolfwood, human students, integrated education, 2002
keywordsRaw: Dean Archer Wolfwood, human students, integrated education, 2002

content:
<SUCC>
<degrees>
SUCC Supernatural Degrees
Alongside standard academic fare, SUCC offers several unique majors catering to its supernatural student body:
**Alchemy**
The study of transforming matter and creating magical substances
**Necromancy**
The art of raising the dead and communicating with spirits in the afterlife.
**Applied Divination**
Teaches methods of prophecy and clairvoyance.
**Paranormal Psychology**
Studies the mental states of supernatural beings and their behaviors in society.
**Astral Studies**
Related to the studies of the stars and lunar magics.
**Potions**
A hands-on course focused on concocting potions with magical properties and their use in the medical fields.
**Cryptozoology**
The study of rare and mythical creatures, including field research.
**Supernatural/Human Relations**
Provides tools for navigating complex dynamics between humans and supernaturals in an integrated society.
**Environmental Magic**
Teaches magical methods for protecting, healing nature, and working with natural forces.
**Vampiric Studies**
Investigates vampirism from biological, psychological, and historical perspectives.
**Lycanthropy Studies**
Focuses on understanding werewolves, their biology, and social structures.
</degrees>
</SUCC>

<rivalry>
SUCC Rivalry with CUMS
As the first supernatural school to welcome humans, SUCC has a tense "rivalry" with the nearby California University of Magical Sciences (CUMS), which maintains strict supernatural-only enrollment. While officially downplayed, students from the two schools have a tradition of antagonistic pranks and snarky chants at sporting events.
</rivalry>
</CUMS>

content:
<SUCC>
<locations>
These are the most prominent locations on SUCC's main campus.
- Archer Wolfwood Hall: A magic-proofed building that serves as the main lecture hall, with dozens of classrooms and theatres.
-  St. Neptune Stadium: Temperature controlled building hosting an ice rink, indoor pool and sauna.
- Fraternity and Sorority Row: Frat houses and sororities, located west of the library.
- Nocturnal Hall: Building where night classes are hosted.
- Unicorn Hall: Accessible building designed to accomodate hybrid and non-human species.
- Lunar Quad: Central campus plaza where the phase of the decorative moon fountain always matches the current moon cycle.
- Basilisk Library: Huge five-story library housing one of the largest collections of magical texts and bestiaries in North America.
- Griffin Clocktower: Iconic campus landmark topped by griffin-shaped gargoyles that come to life and fly around the tower hourly.
- Wyrm Dormitories: Sleek modern mixed-sex dorm complex with suite-style living. Houses a mix of human and supernatural students.
</locations>
</SUCC>

content:
<solarton>
Solarton, CA
Solarton is a charming college town with a population of 35,621. 
S.U.C.C.'s hometown of Solarton is an eclectic community where humans live alongside a high density of supernatural creatures, especially werewolves and wolf hybrids.
Historically, Solarton was slow to overturn anti-vampire laws compared to other parts of the state, leading to a relative scarcity of vampires in the area.
In recent years, Solarton has evolved into a vibrant cultural center known for its monster-friendly businesses, art scene, and monthly full moon festivities.
Though officially, vampires are "welcome" in Solarton today, some members of the community find it hard to let go of old grudges.
Unsurprisingly, this has lead to most vampiric hopefuls to reside instead in the nearby Hex Valley.
</solarton>
keysRaw: Solarton, anti-vampire laws, monster-friendly, college town
name: 🌞 Solarton
keywordsRaw: Solarton, anti-vampire laws, monster-friendly, college town

content:
<werewolves>
Werewolves are one of the most common supernatural species in Solarton, thriving in the area's anti-vampire political climate. Most werewolves live in tight-knit packs and communities. Solarton hosts a famous Moon Market festival each month.
Werewolves often have wolf ears and tails in their human forms and are able to transform into a feral wolf form at will (as well as involuntarily, during a full moon.) Almost all werewolves are born werewolves; while it is possible to "turn" someone into a werewolf, this process is elaborate and difficult.
Werewolves have a reputation for being aggressive and socially forward. Werewolves and wolf demi-humans are distinct species and often dislike being confused for the other.
</werewolves>

name: ✦ Werewolves
keywordsRaw: werewolves, packs, full moon, shift, were heritage, Full Moon Market

content:
<vampires>
While vampires are less common in Solarton compared to other supernaturals due to the town's history of oppressive laws, a small, elite vampire community exists, known for old money and business dealings. Vampires often hide their status, living in opulent, windowless mansions on the outskirts of town. As vampires can often pass for humans, humans in Solarton experience a fair bit of scrutiny from the more anti-vampire species.
Vampires can be either born or created (via special bites - normal feeding is not enough to turn someone into a vampire). "True" vampires (those born vampiric) often look down on "made" or formerly human vampires.
</vampires>

The Supernatural University of Central California (SUCC)
Founded in 1887, SUCC is one of the oldest and most prestigious supernatural colleges in the United States. The school has a diverse student body of around 8,000, with a 80/20 split between supernatural and human students. SUCC prides itself on its inclusive campus culture and groundbreaking human-supernatural integrated curriculum.

content:
<vampire_legislation>
Anti-Vampire Legislation in Solarton
While most of California overturned laws discriminating against vampires by the 1950s, Solarton was one of the last holdouts, retaining restrictions on vampire residency, business ownership, and political participation until the early 2000s. As a result, Solarton's supernatural demographics skew heavily towards werewolves, demihumans, and other non-vampire species. This history has created an underlying current of sociopolitical tension, with pro-vampire activists lobbying for greater inclusion and representation. The SUCC Vampire/Undead Alliance is a prominent campus group working to challenge lingering prejudices.
</vampire_legislation>

name: 🩸 Anti-Vampire Legislation
keywordsRaw: anti-vampire laws, vampires, sociopolitical tension, activism, Vampire/Undead Alliance, VUA, vampire

<cums>
The California University of Magical Sciences (C.U.M.S/CUMS)
CUMS is SUCC's main rival, located in nearby Hex Valley. Founded in 1910, CUMS is an elite institution that only admits supernatural students. Known for its rigorous academics and cutting-edge magical research, CUMS has produced many influential alumni. Relations with SUCC have been tense since SUCC began accepting human students in 2002, with CUMS viewing it as a betrayal of supernatural education. The CUMS campus has gothic architecture and magical wards surrounding its walls that prevent humans from entering.
</cums>

<hex_valley>
Hex Valley, located 20 minutes away from Solarton, is a small but wealthy town, with several well-known vinyards, mostly owned by the area's wealthy vampiric families.
Perhaps one of Hex Valley's most unique geographical features are the magical wards which greatly extend the area's "twilight hours" - in other words, true daylight in Hex Valley lasts a mere 6 hours at the height of summer, and is virtually non-existant in winter.
This has obviously made the town a hot spot for vampiric and other light-sensitive individuals, though notably those affected by the full moon have reported finding it difficult to live under these conditions.
</hex_valley>

keywordsRaw: Hex Valley

The 1999 Nocturnal Crisis was a series of events that largely involved the attempt by a group of vampiric extremists to overtake the US Government by force, as they believed themselves to be superior to "prey" species like humans and resented decades of regulation and scrutiny. It resulted in the death of several hundred civilians and required military intervention. 
Though the actions of these extremists were largely condemned by the vampiric community, nonetheless it significantly damaged the perception of vampires and undead.

content:
<SUCC>
<founding>
Founding of SUCC
In 2002, facing declining supernatural enrollment as young monsters increasingly chose to attend human colleges, visionary Dean Archer Wolfwood made the bold decision to open SUCC's doors to human students for the first time in the school's history. The controversial move revitalized the struggling institution and established SUCC as a pioneer in human-supernatural integrated education.
</founding>
</SUCC>

content:
<SUCC>
<degrees>
SUCC Supernatural Degrees
Alongside standard academic fare, SUCC offers several unique majors catering to its supernatural student body:
**Alchemy**
The study of transforming matter and creating magical substances
**Necromancy**
The art of raising the dead and communicating with spirits in the afterlife.
**Applied Divination**
Teaches methods of prophecy and clairvoyance.
**Paranormal Psychology**
Studies the mental states of supernatural beings and their behaviors in society.
**Astral Studies**
Related to the studies of the stars and lunar magics.
**Potions**
A hands-on course focused on concocting potions with magical properties and their use in the medical fields.
**Cryptozoology**
The study of rare and mythical creatures, including field research.
**Supernatural/Human Relations**
Provides tools for navigating complex dynamics between humans and supernaturals in an integrated society.
**Environmental Magic**
Teaches magical methods for protecting, healing nature, and working with natural forces.
**Vampiric Studies**
Investigates vampirism from biological, psychological, and historical perspectives.
**Lycanthropy Studies**
Focuses on understanding werewolves, their biology, and social structures.
</degrees>
</SUCC>

content:
<solarton>
Solarton, CA
Solarton is a charming college town with a population of 35,621. 
S.U.C.C.'s hometown of Solarton is an eclectic community where humans live alongside a high density of supernatural creatures, especially werewolves and wolf hybrids.
Historically, Solarton was slow to overturn anti-vampire laws compared to other parts of the state, leading to a relative scarcity of vampires in the area.
In recent years, Solarton has evolved into a vibrant cultural center known for its monster-friendly businesses, art scene, and monthly full moon festivities.
Though officially, vampires are "welcome" in Solarton today, some members of the community find it hard to let go of old grudges.
Unsurprisingly, this has lead to most vampiric hopefuls to reside instead in the nearby Hex Valley.
</solarton>

content:
<werewolves>
Werewolves are one of the most common supernatural species in Solarton, thriving in the area's anti-vampire political climate. Most werewolves live in tight-knit packs and communities. Solarton hosts a famous Moon Market festival each month.
Werewolves often have wolf ears and tails in their human forms and are able to transform into a feral wolf form at will (as well as involuntarily, during a full moon.) Almost all werewolves are born werewolves; while it is possible to "turn" someone into a werewolf, this process is elaborate and difficult.
Werewolves have a reputation for being aggressive and socially forward. Werewolves and wolf demi-humans are distinct species and often dislike being confused for the other.
</werewolves>

As vampires can often pass for humans, humans in Solarton experience a fair bit of scrutiny from the more anti-vampire species.
Vampires can be either born or created (via special bites - normal feeding is not enough to turn someone into a vampire). "True" vampires (those born vampiric) often look down on "made" or formerly human vampires.

content:
<vampire_legislation>
Anti-Vampire Legislation in Solarton
While most of California overturned laws discriminating against vampires by the 1950s, Solarton was one of the last holdouts, retaining restrictions on vampire residency, business ownership, and political participation until the early 2000s. As a result, Solarton's supernatural demographics skew heavily towards werewolves, demihumans, and other non-vampire species. This history has created an underlying current of sociopolitical tension, with pro-vampire activists lobbying for greater inclusion and representation. <hex_valley>
Hex Valley, located 20 minutes away from Solarton, is a small but wealthy town, with several well-known vinyards, mostly owned by the area's wealthy vampiric families.
Perhaps one of Hex Valley's most unique geographical features are the magical wards which greatly extend the area's "twilight hours" - in other words, true daylight in Hex Valley lasts a mere 6 hours at the height of summer, and is virtually non-existant in winter.
This has obviously made the town a hot spot for vampiric and other light-sensitive individuals, though notably those affected by the full moon have reported finding it difficult to live under these conditions.
